questions.question: What is the primary goal of coordination in group communication?
questions.explanation: Coordination aims to ensure that all group members are working towards a common goal and are aware of each other's actions.

questions.question: Which of the following coordination mechanisms is used to ensure that only one process can access a shared resource at a time?
questions.explanation: Distributed mutual exclusion is a coordination mechanism that prevents multiple processes from accessing a shared resource simultaneously.

questions.question: What is the purpose of agreement in group communication?
options: C) To ensure that all group members are on the same page
questions.explanation: Agreement in group communication ensures that all members have a shared understanding of the task, goals, and expectations.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a distributed agreement mechanism?
questions.explanation: A voting system is a distributed agreement mechanism where group members vote on a decision, and the outcome is agreed upon.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using coordination and agreement mechanisms in group communication?
questions.explanation: Using coordination and agreement mechanisms in group communication leads to improved communication, cooperation, and ultimately, better outcomes.
